prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear, the CPU is like the brain of your computer! It’s the little part that thinks really hard and tells all the other pieces what to do, kind of like how I tell you to finish your veggies. It takes the instructions you give—like typing or clicking—and makes everything happen, just like how my hands knit your scarf when my brain says so. It works super fast, much faster than old me, but in a way, it’s just doing the thinking so the computer can help you learn or play. Without it, your computer would be like a car with no engine—just sitting there, not going anywhere. Neat, huh?
